meta.title: LLM Evals - Part 2: Improving Performance
meta.description:
➡️ Lifetime access to the ADVANCED-Evals Repo (incl. future additions): https://trelis.com/ADVANCED-evals/
➡️ Slides: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1qhfxURAJFP81HCmefyELq33KFmnBW5bEyMklc5s6Ww0/edit?usp=sharing
➡️ Thumbnail made with this tutorial: https://youtu.be/ThKYjTdkyP8

OTHER TRELIS LINKS:
➡️ Trelis Newsletter: https://trelis.substack.com
➡️ Other Resources and Products from Trelis: https://Trelis.com/

TIMESTAMPS:
00:00:00 - Introduction to LLM evals and their importance
00:00:15 - Overview of creating high-quality prompts from scratch
00:00:32 - Emphasis on importance of high-quality examples
00:00:57 - Introduction to systematic approach for creating examples
00:01:14 - Overview of demonstration using touch rugby example
00:02:23 - Introduction to LLM eval repo and UI demonstration
00:03:30 - Start of UI demonstration with pipeline creation
00:04:39 - Creating initial pipeline with Claude Sonnet
00:05:54 - Creating first dataset for touch rugby Q&A
00:07:47 - Setting up evaluation criteria and format requirements
00:09:04 - Demonstration of generating ground truth answers
00:10:14 - Creating second evaluation task
00:12:14 - Introduction to creating few-shot examples
00:14:15 - Setting up pipeline with few-shot examples
00:19:21 - Creating training examples for few-shot learning
00:23:59 - Demonstration of improved performance with few-shot examples
00:27:53 - Discussion of pipeline customization options
00:28:28 - Final tips on judges and evaluation
00:29:56 - Recommendations for managing examples
00:31:30 - Discussion of OpenAI's o1 model considerations
00:34:23 - Conclusion and future topics
